glossary..toe: TOuch Environment file, the file type used by TouchDesigner to save your entire project.
glossary..tox: TouchDesigner Component file, the file type used to save a Component of your TouchDesigner project.
glossary.Absolute Time: Absolute Time starts counting from 0 when the TouchDesigner process starts, and is always increasing.
glossary.Adaptive Homing: Option in Node Viewer that continually keeps in-view the 3D geometry being displayed, even when the geometry changes shape, size and animated position.
glossary.Annotate: Annotates are displayed in the Network Editor as colored rectangles containing user-authored text and graphics.
glossary.Attributes: Data associated with POP geometry. Points, Vertex and Primitives can have any number of attributes.
glossary.Backdrop: The grid of node viewers that are visible behind the Network, set by turning on Display Flags.
glossary.Binding: Parameter Mode that ties two or more parameters values together, where changing the value of any one of the bound parameters changes all of them.
glossary.Bookmarks: A pull-down list at the top of a network Pane containing jump-to Network Paths.
glossary.Bypass Flag: An operator whose Bypass flag is set does nothing: All data going into its first input appears at its output unaffected.
glossary.Callback: Some operators have a DAT docked to them that contains python functions called when something in the operator changes.
glossary.Channel: A CHOP outputs one or more channels, where a channel is simply a sequence of numbers (Samples).
glossary.CHOP: Channel Operator - Operator Family which operate on Channels (a sequence of numbers) used for animation, audio, mathematics, simulation, logic.
glossary.Clone: Cloning makes multiple components match the contents of a master component.
glossary.Component: Operator Family that contains its own Network. There are sixteen 3D Object Component and ten 2D Panel Component types.
glossary.Connector: The notches on the left and right of each Node that let you Wire the output of one Operator to the input of another.
glossary.Container: The Container component type is a Panel Component that holds, lays out and displays any number of other Panel Components.
glossary.Cook: To re-compute the output data of the Operators. An operator cooks when its inputs change, Parameters change, timeline moves forward, or Scripting commands run.
glossary.DAT: Data Operator - Operator Family that manipulates text strings: multi-line text or tables.
glossary.Dependency: The Procedural mechanism in TouchDesigner, where if one piece of data changes, it automatically causes other operators and expressions to re-Cook.
glossary.Designer Mode: You edit your networks in Designer Mode. See Perform Mode.
glossary.Display Flag: The blue flag on Geometry components and SOP operators determines if the geometry contained in that operator is visible.
glossary.Docking: Any node can be docked to another node. This helps organize networks as two nodes that are docked together will stay together.
glossary.Event: Events are single-moment occurrences that are generated from a variety of conditions - from input actions, external devices, and internal TouchDesigner states.
glossary.Export: Exporting is the connection of CHOP channels to parameters of operators.
glossary.Expression: A text string that contains data and operators that are evaluated by the node's language (python or Tscript).
glossary.Extensions: Any component can be extended with its own Python classes which contain python functions and data.
glossary.Filter: Operators that need 1 or more inputs are called Filters in TouchDesigner, like a Math CHOP.
glossary.Flag: Indicator of certain states of an operator (bypass, display, lock, viewer active).
glossary.Frame: The term Frame is a measurement of time used in the Timeline, as a time-unit in CHOPs, and as a time unit in movie files.
glossary.Frame Rate: The Frames-per-Second that TouchDesigner's Timeline runs at. Set with project.cookRate.
glossary.Generator: Operators that do not need any inputs connected are called Generators in TouchDesigner, like a Pattern CHOP.
glossary.Geometry: The 3D data held in SOPs and passed for rendering by the Geometry COMP.
glossary.Global OP Shortcut: A name for a component that is accessible from any node in a project.
glossary.GPU: The Graphics Processing Unit. This is the high-speed, many-core processor of the graphics card/chip.
glossary.Group: A Group in POPs and SOPs is a named subset of points or primitives.
glossary.Hierarchy: Hierarchy relates components with other components. There are two groups of Hierarchy in TouchDesigner: 3D Object Components, and 2D Panel Components.
glossary.Instance: A Geometry Component can instance and render its SOP geometry many times. An instance is an OP that doesnt actually have its own data.
glossary.Internal Operators: Internal Operators provide a simple shortcut to a frequently-used operator, accessing from anywhere in that component.
glossary.Internal Parameters: Internal Parameters act like local variables in a component.
glossary.Keyframe: In the Animation component each keyframe specifies a channels value at a specific time (or frame).
glossary.Lock Flag: When an operator is locked, it does not cook and its output data remains frozen.
glossary.MAT: Materials are an Operator Family that applies a Shader to a SOP or 3D Geometry Object for rendering.
glossary.Network: Every component contains a network of operators that create and modify data.
glossary.Network Editor: A pane type where networks of operators can be created and edited.
glossary.Node: The generic thing that holds an Operator, and includes Flags and its position/size in the network.
glossary.Node Viewer: The viewer found on each operator in a Network Editor pane.
glossary.Operator: Any of the procedural data operators. OPs do all the work in TouchDesigner.
glossary.Operator Family: The Operator Families are TOPs, CHOPs, POPs, MATs, DATs, SOPs and Components.
glossary.Palette: A built-in panel in TouchDesigner that contains a library of components and media.
glossary.Pane: A work area in TouchDesigner's layout that includes the Network Editor and 7 other pane types.
glossary.Panel Component: The sub-Family of Components that are used to create custom interactive 2D control panels.
glossary.Panel Value: The internal states of a panel component are Panel Values, accessed with a Panel CHOP or Python expression.
glossary.Parameter: Every operator in TouchDesigner has a set of control Parameters that can be integer or floating point numbers, menus, binary toggles, text strings.
glossary.Parameter Mode: Every Parameter can be in one of four modes: Constant Mode, Expression Mode, Export Mode or Bind Mode.
glossary.Parent Shortcut: A Parent Shortcut is a parameter on a component that contains a name to refer to that component using the syntax parent.Name.
glossary.Path: The location of an operator within the TouchDesigner environment, for example, /geo1/circle1.
glossary.Pattern Matching: Matching names using wildcard characters and bracketing. Useful in Select type parameters.
glossary.Perform Mode: Perform Mode is an optimized mode for live performance that only renders one specified Window COMP.
glossary.Performance Monitor: The tool built-in to TouchDesigner that analyzes and displays what TouchDesigner is doing.
glossary.Point: Each SOP has a list of Points. Each point has an XYZ 3D position value plus other optional attributes.
glossary.Polygon: A polygon is a type of Primitive that is formed from a set of Vertices in 3D.
glossary.POP: Point Operators - POPs is a new Operator Family of TouchDesigner that runs on the GPU, creates/modifies 3D data which is rendered by the Render TOP.
glossary.Primitive: A surface type in SOPs that includes polygon, curve (NURBS and Bezier), patch and other basic shapes.
glossary.Procedural: Procedural means the automatic generation of outputs based on live inputs and the current state of TouchDesigner.
glossary.Projection Mapping: A technique or workflow that allows for displaying content on often irregular shapes and surfaces.
glossary.Pulse: To pulse a parameter is to send it a signal that causes a new action to occur immediately.
glossary.Reference: The grey dashed lines between nodes is a Reference (or Link).
glossary.Render Flag: The purple flag on COMP and SOP nodes that determines if the node will be rendered.
glossary.Rendering: Rendering is the creation of a 3D image with the Render TOP.
glossary.Replicator: Creates copies of a component, one for every row in a table. It is the for-loop of operators.
glossary.Resolution: The width and height of an image in pixels.
glossary.Root: TouchDesigner is a hierarchy of components. root is the top-most network in the hierarchy.
glossary.Sample Rate: Samples-per-second of a CHOP. Each CHOP in your network has a sample rate.
glossary.Scope: A parameter in most CHOPs that restricts which channels of that CHOP will be affected.
glossary.Script: A set of commands located in a Text DAT that are triggered to run under certain conditions.
glossary.Shader: The OpenGL or Vulkan code that runs on the GPU and creates rendered images.
glossary.Shortcut: Operator shortcuts are Python objects that return operators (or sometimes parameters).
glossary.Snippets: OP Snippets is a set of 700+ live examples of TouchDesigner operators.
glossary.SOP: Surface Operator - Operator Family that reads, creates and modifies 3D points, polygons, lines, particles, surfaces, spheres and meatballs.
glossary.Storage: Storage is a python dictionary in each operator, where users can store and fetch extra data.
glossary.Table DAT: A form of DATs that is structured as rows and columns of text strings.
glossary.Tag: Each operator can have a set of text strings that are its tags.
glossary.Time Slice: A Time Slice is the time from the last cook frame to the current cook frame.
glossary.Timeline: The panel at the bottom of TouchDesigner, it controls the current global looping Time.
glossary.TOP: Texture Operator - Operator Family that creates, composites and modifies images.
glossary.Vertex: A sequence of vertices form a Polygon in a SOP.
glossary.VFS: Virtual File System - Lets you embed files inside a .tox or .toe file.
glossary.Viewer: The viewer of a node can be the interior of a node, a floating window, or a Pane.
glossary.Viewer Active: A state of a node where you can operate the contents of its viewer.
glossary.Widgets: Widgets is a diverse collection of components located in the Palette, designed for building user interfaces.
glossary.Wire: The connection of an output of one node to the input of another node in a network.
